title: All your emails in Gmail
body:
![STOP the SPAM](http://farm3.static.flickr.com/2047/1900798071_4a2fb1980c.jpg?v=1194441333)I have several domains on various *hosting* services and email accounts on all of them, and every day I needed more time to check all the inboxes—especially since I’ve gotten used to using only *WebMail* (since I work from different computers). Plus, some *hosting* services have storage limits, and my inboxes were filling up with SPAM. To solve this, I decided to **consolidate all my accounts into my *Gmail***, following these steps:

1.  After emptying all the inboxes, I converted the accounts into *mail forwards* so that they all point to a single *Gmail* account.
2.  In the /accounts/ tab of the *Gmail* settings menu, I added these now-redirected email addresses and checked the box for /Reply from the same address the message was sent from/.

By doing this, I’ve achieved:

1.  Receive all emails from all my accounts in my Gmail without anyone who receives or sends emails to or from any of my accounts noticing any apparent change.
2.  When composing messages, I can choose the source account I want (in the "From:" field).
3.  Filter out all SPAM at once, thanks to Google *Gmail*’s powerful filters.

I’ve also set up a series of filters to distinguish the *emails* from each account. The only thing I’ve found missing is the ability to set up a different signature for each account; I hope this will be possible in future updates to *Gmail* (which is still in beta).
